title: Next gameplay phase: field-season routes and a stronger medium-term play loop
summary: Twenty-third structured packet for Eco Explorer. The queue is clear again because the first guided field-season loop, second traversal-heavy biome, second-tier station upgrade, and first visible process moment all landed successfully. The next leverage is no longer another isolated mechanic. It is a calm route-progression layer centered on the field station: one small `field season` board, one authored cross-biome route, then more route-aligned content fuel, a treeline traversal shelter proof, one additional utility reward, and only then broader inland process moments.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the act-two beat sheet and route matrix for the first field-season board.
execution_order.why_now: The main pass should land one authored route, not invent route structure on the fly.

execution_order.goal: Implement the field-season board and route progression v1 at the field station.
execution_order.why_now: This is the highest-leverage way to make the game feel longer and more intentional without changing its genre.

execution_order.goal: Review the first route layer for cohesion, readability, and whether it feels notebook-first rather than quest-log-heavy.
execution_order.why_now: The route shell should be critiqued before more content and traversal work are shaped around it.

execution_order.goal: Add a route-aligned content fuel pack across the live coastal branch and the first inland continuation.
execution_order.why_now: The route layer will only feel authored if it has enough discoveries, notes, and comparison anchors to feed it.

execution_order.goal: Review the route-aligned content pack for science quality, pacing, and whether it makes the season board feel fuller instead of busier.
execution_order.why_now: The route should stay focused and readable even as the authored support grows.

execution_order.goal: Deepen treeline with a short sheltered traversal proof that supports the next route phase.
execution_order.why_now: A second strong inland traversal beat expands the world’s feel more than another small overlay or side feature.

execution_order.goal: Review the treeline shelter proof for ecology teaching, movement readability, and kid-friendly difficulty.
execution_order.why_now: Treeline is the right next movement biome, but it is also easy to over-harden if not checked.

execution_order.goal: Add one route reward or utility unlock that makes later season work feel meaningful without becoming a market loop.
execution_order.why_now: The field station can support one more reward only after the route and inland traversal pillars are holding.

execution_order.goal: Review the new route reward for ethics, clarity, and whether it stays observational rather than extractive or grindy.
execution_order.why_now: Reward growth is high leverage but easy to push out of tone if it is not checked.

execution_order.goal: Expand inland process moments and route revisit support once the route shell and reward layer are stable.
execution_order.why_now: Forest, treeline, and tundra revisit change will land much harder after the player has a stronger season structure.

guardrails: Science accuracy remains a hard gate.
Do not respond to a clear queue with random polish or disconnected microfeatures.
The next phase should feel like a stronger medium-term play loop, not a second dashboard.
Route progression should stay field-station-centered and notebook-first.
Do not build a large quest log, checklist screen, or score-heavy task system.
New rewards should help observation, comfort, or traversal instead of teaching extraction or selling wildlife.
Treeline traversal should stay short, sheltered, and forgiving.
Inland process moments should show ecology through visible habitat change, not generic visual effects.
acceptance_focus: The queue is no longer blank and points at one coherent next phase.
The next main lane makes the game feel longer and more intentional without changing its tone.
Scout work can continue in parallel without blocking the main route-progression implementation.
Later traversal, reward, and inland-process work now have a stable medium-term progression shell to land on.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-next-high-leverage-phase.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-functional-gameplay-sequence.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-guided-field-season-beat-sheet.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-world-continuity-preproduction-pack.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-field-season-routes-phase.md, and this packet. Treat the now-clear queue as a sign to move from a strong first guided loop into a stronger medium-term route-progression phase. The first live move is a field-season board with one authored cross-biome route, not another unrelated feature drop.
